headline: Truist Financial (TFC) Names Michael Lyons CEO In Its First Leadership Handover
summary: Truist Financial (NYSE:TFC) has appointed Michael P. Lyons as its new CEO and President. Lyons succeeds Bill Rogers, marking the first CEO transition since Truist Financial was formed. The leadership change places a former Fiserv and PNC executive at the top of the bank, with its shares trading at US$48.48. Truist Financial enters this leadership change with its stock trading at US$48.48 and a one-year return of 28.7%. The company also shows a five-year return of 13.0% and a value score of...

headline: Fiserv (FISV) Stock Valuation After CEO Exit And Activist Pressure
summary: CEO shakeup and activist pressure put Fiserv (FISV) in focus Fiserv (FISV) is back on investors’ radar after CEO Mike Lyons resigned to lead Truist Financial, with Takis Georgakopoulos taking over as chief executive and joining the board. The leadership change comes alongside ongoing pressure from activist investor Jana Partners and follows Fiserv’s decision to reaffirm its 2026 guidance. This has kept attention on how the new CEO might approach the company’s payment and financial technology...

headline: Truist Financial (TFC) Stock After 28.7% One Year Return Is Valuation Still Attractive
summary: Wondering if Truist Financial at around US$48.48 is giving you fair value or a potential discount? This article breaks down what the current price might really imply. The stock has slipped about 0.7% over the last week but is up 3.2% over the past month, while the 1 year return sits at 28.7% and the 3 year return at 77.3%, with a 5 year return of 13.0%. Recent attention on Truist Financial has been driven by ongoing coverage of US regional banks, regulatory developments affecting the sector,...
